content.claim: Entrance examinations to upper-secondary schools in Hungary are centrally organised through a standardised national process managed by the Ministry of Culture and Innovation, prioritizing primary school grades and entrance exams
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Describes administrative mechanism, not its effect on equity outcomes
Applies to all upper-secondary tracks, whether 6/8-year extended or standard 4-year programs
Does not indicate whether centralization increases or decreases selection pressure
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
